summary: The per-kind discovery scan unified onto the tolerant shared walk; `ImportError::ReadDir` is now unconstructable and six `discover_*` fns carry vacuous `Result` — collapse to bare collections

edit.description: The residue of the retired second discovery path. `read_entries` (the only `ImportError::ReadDir` constructor) was retired at 0bc0ee9 when the per-kind scan moved onto the shared walk's in-memory index; `ImportError::ReadDir` (variant at 142, in the enum at 138) is now unconstructable — grep finds no builder in src/ or tests/ — leaving `ImportError::Write` (153, built only at 660/668 by the roll-up writer) the sole live variant. Retire the `ReadDir` variant (this is a symbol removal within a surviving file, not a file retirement — the file stays, so it is an edit, not a `files.retire` entry). Then collapse the six vacuous-`Result` discovery fns to their bare collection: `discover_builtin` (206, `-> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, ImportError>`), `discover_nested_file` (252, `-> Result<Vec<NestedFileUnit>, ImportError>`), `declared_governed_paths` (304, `-> Result<BTreeSet<PathBuf>, ImportError>`), `discover_kind_files` (358), `discover_kind_units` (393), `scan_locus` (417) — none can `Err`: the shared walk swallows I/O via `walk.flatten()` (548, the settled tolerance posture since f419987) and `scan_locus`/`collect_glob` now read the in-memory index and touch no filesystem. Drop each internal `?`. `ImportError` (doc comment at 137, 'discovering or rolling up') is now Write-only — reword the doc to the writer alone, or rename the type to a writer-scoped error (a judgment call for the build; name it in the commit body). The inline `discover_builtin_*` tests (790+) drop their `.unwrap()`. Addresses re-verified at HEAD 1126033 (unmoved since 04b33de — the only commit in between, 1126033, touched `.flume/**` alone).

The ripple past import.rs. The manifest error enum's `Discovery(#[from] crate::import::ImportError)` variant (316) becomes unconstructable once `discover_kind_files` (called at 431, currently `?`-ed) is infallible — json_manifest only reads members, never writes, so no `ImportError` reaches it. Drop the `?` at 431 and the now-dead `Discovery` variant (and its `#[from]`), or narrow the enum to whatever error the reader still raises. Re-verified at HEAD 1126033.

Two `?` sites drop when discovery is infallible: `discover_nested_file` (1353) and `discover_kind_files` (1372). After removing them, re-check whether main's error surface still names `ImportError` at all here; leave nothing wrapping a variant that can no longer flow. Re-verified at HEAD 1126033.

The `discover_builtin` call `?`-ed at 332 drops its `?`. Same re-check as main.rs — install never writes through this path, so no `ImportError` reaches it after the collapse. Re-verified at HEAD 1126033.

tests.asserts: the measure-first cost fixture still pins read_dir_count == 0 with walk_count/glob_compile_count unchanged — the collapse is signature-only, discovery behavior byte-identical
the inline discover_builtin_* tests yield the same member sets with the infallible signatures (unwraps removed); no ImportError::ReadDir and no Result remain in the discovery read path

acceptance: The discovery read path is infallible end to end — `discover_builtin`/`discover_nested_file`/`declared_governed_paths`/`discover_kind_files`/`discover_kind_units`/`scan_locus` return bare collections, `ImportError::ReadDir` is gone, and `ImportError` (or its writer-scoped rename) carries only the roll-up writer's failure. No behavior change (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': a retired second implementation's residue is subtracted, not carried). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Drained from .flume/refactor/build-discovery-infallible-result-plumbing.md (observed 8b06146, cut 0bc0ee9). Pure subtraction, no 'Loud or nothing' fork — flatten() tolerance settled pre-SCAN (f419987), discovery was already infallible. Refile of 648fba5, reverted: its `files.retire` named a symbol string, not a path, tripping the fence gate. Fixed: ReadDir removal is now prose inside the import.rs edit, retire: []. scoped at 1126033.

summary: frontmatter.rs's test fixtures pull real skill/rule Kind values via builtin_kind::definition — a provider dependency in a format adapter's tests; swap to synthetic test_support kinds (0040)

edit.description: Test module (466-478) imports `crate::builtin_kind` and its `skill_kind()`/`rule_kind()` helpers call `builtin_kind::definition("skill"/"rule")` for real CustomKind values — the test-only `frontmatter → builtin_kind` edge architecture.md declares (0040, decision point 2). Swap both helpers to call a new test_support kind-builder instead, dropping the `crate::builtin_kind` import. The synthetic kinds must reproduce the exact shape the 8 tests below rely on: `skill_kind()` — `Format::YamlFrontmatter`, `UnitShape::Directory`, declared fields in order `name`, `description`, `license` (mirrors `builtin_kind::skill_extraction`, builtin_kind.rs:25-41's first three `Primitive::Field`s — the fixture's `allowed-tools`/`priority` are deliberately undeclared, asserted as sorted-after-declared unknown keys, so the builder must NOT declare them); `rule_kind()` — `Format::YamlFrontmatter`, `UnitShape::File`, one declared field `paths` (mirrors `builtin_kind::claude_code_rule`, builtin_kind.rs:187-190).

Add the synthetic kind-builder decision 0040 names (currently one fn, `tmpdir` at 7) — a `pub(crate)` constructor(s) building a minimal `CustomKind` via `CustomKind::new(name, governs, extraction)` (kind.rs:747) for a directory-shaped and a file-shaped frontmatter fixture, parametrized by declared field names in order plus unit_shape, sufficient for frontmatter.rs's `skill_kind()`/`rule_kind()` to stop importing `builtin_kind`. Real governs/format details are test-arbitrary (`Member::from_source` takes an explicit path, never discovery), so only the extraction/unit_shape/format axes need to match production.

tests.asserts: all 8 existing inline tests (directory_shape_ids_..., file_shape_ids_..., companions_scan_..., a_non_mapping_frontmatter_..., a_malformed_yaml_frontmatter_..., an_empty_frontmatter_..., a_paths_rule_lifts_...) pass unchanged against the synthetic kinds — same field order, same id derivation, same error behavior

acceptance: src/frontmatter.rs's test module carries no `crate::builtin_kind` import; its fixtures build kinds through a `test_support` kind-builder instead. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Decision 0040, point 2. Disjoint from every other queue entry's files (frontmatter.rs, test_support.rs touched nowhere else this tick). scoped at f404e48.

summary: roster.rs (model) imports crate::builtin (provider) solely for kind_narrowing_clause, a generic clause-builder with no Claude Code dependency — relocate it to compose.rs, Requirement's own home

edit.description: `kind_narrowing_clause` (50-76, doc + fn) builds an ordinary `Clause`/`Predicate::Kind` from two `&str` arguments — no `crate::builtin_lock` or Claude-Code-specific data touches it, so its home in this provider module is architecture.md's stated violation ('the provider face is data the engine loads, never a dependency of the model'): `crate::roster` (model subsystem) has to import this provider module just to reach it. Move the fn (with its doc comment) to `src/compose.rs`, beside `Requirement` (the noun the fn's own doc says it narrows). After the move, `Predicate`/`Severity` and the bare `contract::` self-import become dead in this file — `kind_narrowing_clause` was their only user (grep confirms no other `Predicate`/`Severity`/bare-`contract::` reference in builtin.rs) — so the import line (15) narrows to `use crate::contract::{Clause, Contract};`.

Gains `kind_narrowing_clause(requirement: &str, kind: &str) -> contract::Clause` (moved verbatim from builtin.rs, doc comment included), rewritten to use this file's existing qualified-path style (`contract::Clause { .. }`, `contract::Predicate::Kind { .. }`, `contract::Severity::Required`, `contract::clause_label`, `contract::requirement_owner`) rather than adding new unqualified imports — `compose.rs` already imports `use crate::contract::{self, Contract};` and uses exactly this qualified style elsewhere (`clause_from_row`, `severity_from_label`).

Drop `use crate::builtin;` (14). The three `builtin::kind_narrowing_clause` references — the doc comment at 57, the real call at 79 inside `selections`, the doc comment at 104 — become `compose::kind_narrowing_clause`, folding into the existing `use crate::compose::{Requirement, Verifier};` import (already present) as `use crate::compose::{kind_narrowing_clause, Requirement, Verifier};`.

Four references to `temper::builtin::kind_narrowing_clause` — the doc comment at 239 and the call at 244 (`the_kind_narrowing_clause_loads_at_required_severity`), the doc comment at 260 and the call at 261 (`the_kind_narrowing_clause_round_trips_in_a_requirements_clause_set`) — become `temper::compose::kind_narrowing_clause`.

tests.asserts: the_kind_narrowing_clause_loads_at_required_severity and the_kind_narrowing_clause_round_trips_in_a_requirements_clause_set pass unchanged calling temper::compose::kind_narrowing_clause
roster.rs's own inline tests (selections()/judge_roster over a requirement with a kind facet) pass unchanged — the move is location-only, no behavior change

acceptance: `src/roster.rs` carries no `crate::builtin` import; `kind_narrowing_clause` lives in `src/compose.rs`; `src/builtin.rs`'s import list drops the now-dead `Predicate`/`Severity`/bare `contract::` self-import. architecture.md's invariant ('the provider face is ... never a dependency of the model') holds for roster.rs. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping 'model' (foundation done, model this tick). roster.rs's separate `crate::engine` import stays untouched — doc-justified delegation to the judge algebra, and architecture.md states no model-never-depends-on-judges invariant, unlike the provider one this fixes; surfaced for a human read, not filed. Disjoint from every other entry this tick. scoped at 4adb1fb.

summary: document.rs's `+++`-fenced Document/DocumentError type and its add_clause/add_satisfies/add_provenance/clauses/provenance helpers are zero-consumer residue from 664a522

edit.description: Grep-verified zero-consumer outside this file's own `mod tests` (checked src/, tests/, sdk/): the `Document` struct (31-48 doc+struct) and its `impl Document` block (105-196: `new`/`parse`/`emit`/`header`/`header_mut`/`body`), `DocumentError` (50-103), `child_table` (220-234), `add_clause` (236-243), `add_satisfies` (245-252), `add_provenance` (254-272), `clauses` (274-293), `provenance` (347-355), `is_fence` (357-362), `parse_header` (364-380), `header_span` (382-390), and the `FENCE` const (27-29) — all residue from 664a522 ('retire the pre-0016 own-path surface-document mechanism'), which removed every real caller of this `+++`-fenced surface-document machinery (`surface_overlay`, `Member::from_surface`/`to_document`) but left the machinery itself standing, exercised only by its own inline tests. `Satisfies`/`Satisfies::new` (198-217 — live consumers: frontmatter.rs, read.rs, kind.rs, builtin_kind.rs, main.rs) and `item_to_json`/`value_to_json` (295-345 — item_to_json's live consumer: toml_document.rs) are this file's only real surface; keep both verbatim, remove every other item named above. Narrow imports accordingly: drop `use std::path::Path;` (19, only add_provenance's caller) and `use miette::SourceSpan;` (21, only DocumentError); narrow `use toml_edit::{DocumentMut, Item, Table, Value};` (23) to `use toml_edit::{Item, Value};` (Item/Value are item_to_json/value_to_json's own types; DocumentMut/Table were Document-only); drop `use crate::drift;` (25, only add_provenance's `to_lock_path` call). Reword the module doc comment (1-17), which describes only the retired `+++`-fenced 'surface document' concept, to state the file's real remaining job — the `satisfies` clause type plus the `Item`/`Value` → JSON conversion `toml_document.rs` shares. In `mod tests` (392-613), delete the 13 tests that exercise only the retired surface (parse_then_emit_is_byte_identical, body_is_verbatim, empty_header_round_trips, header_patch_preserves_comments_order_and_whitespace, unknown_header_tables_are_preserved_verbatim, missing_opening_fence_is_a_precise_error, empty_input_is_a_missing_fence_error, unterminated_fence_is_a_precise_error, bare_opening_fence_is_unterminated_not_a_panic, malformed_header_toml_is_a_precise_error, clause_modules_emit_as_labelled_tables_and_round_trip, add_provenance_normalizes_a_backslash_joined_source_path, new_uses_canonical_fences_and_round_trips) plus the now-unused FIXTURE const (400-414) if nothing else reads it. Rewrite `item_to_json_converts_each_clause_value_kind_faithfully` (578-601) to reach its per-value-kind (string/int/bool/array/inline-table) `[clause.*]` fixture by parsing the TOML text directly into a `toml_edit::DocumentMut` and walking its `clause` table, rather than through the retired `Document::parse`/`clauses` helpers — same fixture text, same assertions on item_to_json's output.

tests.asserts: item_to_json_converts_each_clause_value_kind_faithfully passes after its rewrite off the retired Document/clauses path, with the same per-value-kind coverage of item_to_json's output
no test in the file's mod tests references Document, DocumentError, add_clause, add_satisfies, add_provenance, clauses, or provenance — the retired surface carries no surviving assertion

acceptance: src/document.rs carries no Document/DocumentError/add_clause/add_satisfies/add_provenance/clauses/provenance/child_table/FENCE — only Satisfies and the Item/Value→JSON conversion (item_to_json/value_to_json) remain, both unchanged. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping the `formats` subsystem (posture rotation, formats next after model). frontmatter.rs's provider dependency is already queued (FRONTMATTER-TEST-SYNTHETIC-KINDS); json_manifest.rs's dead `Discovery` variant is already queued (DISCOVERY-INFALLIBLE-RESULT-COLLAPSE); toml_document.rs is clean. Disjoint from every other queue entry's files. scoped at 15bf420.

summary: read.rs's impact/context/requirements/field explain-strands are pub with zero consumer outside explain() in the same file — narrow to private, matching why's earned pub

edit.description: Four `pub fn`s carry no caller outside this file: `impact` (593), `context` (856), `requirements` (1254), `field` (1468) — each documented in its own doc comment as `explain`'s named strand (module doc 10-17: 'requirements walks it in reverse', 'impact narrates the blast radius', 'context emits the declared neighborhood') and called only from `explain` (218, the sole dispatcher) within this same file. Grep-verified zero hits for `read::impact`/`read::context`/`read::requirements`/`read::field` across src/, tests/, sdk/; `tests/read_verbs.rs` (the file's own integration coverage) calls `read::why` (353, 554, 583) and `read::explain` (89) directly but never these four — unlike `why` (320), which earns its `pub` through those real external callers, these four's only reachable path from outside the module is through `explain`'s dispatch. The file's only inline test module, `impact_tests` (1552), exercises `impact` from within the same file — an in-module test, per the KIND-ZERO-CONSUMER-EXPORTS-PRUNE precedent ('outside kind.rs's own inline tests'), does not earn a wider-than-needed visibility. Narrow `impact`, `context`, `requirements`, and `field` from `pub fn` to module-private `fn` — no `pub(crate)` needed, no sibling module calls them. Leave `why` and `explain` (both real exports) untouched, and leave the intra-doc links (`[requirements]`/`[impact]`/`[context]`/`[field]`) as-is — they resolve identically at private visibility since every reference sits within this same file.

tests.asserts: existing explain()/why() coverage stays green — the narrowing is visibility-only, explain's composed output over impact/context/requirements/field is unchanged
impact_tests (1552) keeps passing calling impact() from within the same module at its new private visibility

acceptance: impact, context, requirements, and field are private fns in src/read.rs, not pub; why and explain keep their pub visibility. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping the `pipeline` subsystem (posture rotation: pipeline this tick, after formats). Disjoint from every other queue entry's files (read.rs touched nowhere else). scoped at 20b7b21.

summary: engine.rs's Selector::label is pub but called only from finding() in the same file — zero outside consumer; narrow to private per the export-earns-its-consumer bar

edit.description: `Selector::label` (424-433, `pub fn label(&self) -> &str`) has exactly one call site in the whole tree — `finding()` (685-693), inside this same file, at line 689 — grep-verified zero hits for `.label()` on a `Selector` or `Selector::label` anywhere else in src/, tests/, sdk/. Its sibling `Selector::noun()` (438-444) is legitimately `pub` — its own doc comment (435-437) names the real cross-module consumer, `crate::graph::degree`'s findings — which is exactly the earned-`pub` shape `label` lacks: nothing outside this module reads it, and no doc comment names a planned second consumer. Drop `pub` from `label`, leaving it a module-private method callable from `finding()` unchanged.

tests.asserts: existing engine.rs inline tests over finding()/wrong_kind() and every selection-grain diagnostic stay green — the narrowing is visibility-only, finding()'s output (the diagnostic's artifact field) is unchanged

acceptance: `Selector::label` is a private method in src/engine.rs, not `pub`; `Selector::noun` keeps its earned `pub`. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green (no unused-pub warning survives).
notes: Found sweeping the `judges` subsystem (posture rotation: pipeline done, judges this tick). Disjoint from every other queue entry's files (engine.rs touched nowhere else this tick). scoped at e2325df.

summary: reporter.rs's github and sarif each independently match Severity::Error/Warn to the literal words "error"/"warning" — the same normalizer written twice in one file

edit.description: `github` (173-199) and `sarif` (215-...) each carry their own copy of the identical match: `Severity::Error => "error", Severity::Warn => "warning"` — `github`'s at 184-187 (bound to `command`, used for the `::error`/`::warning` workflow-command name) and `sarif`'s at 219-222 (bound to `level`, used for SARIF's `level` field). Both read `diagnostic.severity` from `crate::check::Severity` and produce the exact same two-word mapping — the 'two normalizers doing the same thing' residue class, harmless only because GitHub's command name and SARIF's `level` value happen to coincide today. Add a private `fn severity_word(severity: Severity) -> &'static str` in this file (returning `"error"`/`"warning"` over the same two-arm match) and call it from both `github` (replacing 184-187) and `sarif` (replacing 219-222).

tests.asserts: github_emits_one_workflow_command_line_per_finding_with_the_rule_as_title, sarif_is_valid_json_with_the_temper_driver_and_one_result_per_diagnostic, and both_reporters_are_pure_presentations_of_the_diagnostic_set stay green — the consolidation is signature-only, both renderers' severity words are unchanged

acceptance: `github` and `sarif` both call one shared `severity_word` fn in src/reporter.rs instead of each carrying its own `Severity => &str` match. No behavior change (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': a second implementation of one job is residue, subtracted rather than carried). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping the `judges` subsystem (posture rotation: pipeline done, judges this tick). Disjoint from every other queue entry's files (reporter.rs touched nowhere else this tick). scoped at e2325df.

summary: builtin_kind::qualified is pub with zero consumer outside its own inline test — prune per the export-earns-its-consumer bar

edit.description: `qualified` (508-522, doc comment 512-519 plus fn) has zero consumer anywhere in src/, tests/, sdk/ outside its own inline test — grep-verified: no `builtin_kind::qualified` call and no bare `qualified(` call anywhere in src/, tests/, sdk/src/, sdk/test/. Its neighbor `definition` (501-509) is the real accessor every real call site uses (main.rs, install.rs, bundle.rs, import.rs, frontmatter.rs, a dozen tests/*.rs files); `qualified`'s own doc comment concedes it as a documented-but-unadopted convenience ('kept for call sites that ask for a kind's identity rather than its full definition') — no call site ever adopted it. Remove the fn (520-522) and its doc comment (512-519). In `mod tests`, remove `qualified_names_every_embedded_kind_by_its_own_bare_name` (758-775), the fn's only caller.

tests.asserts: every other builtin_kind.rs inline test (definition/definitions/features coverage) stays green — the removal is dead-code-only, no behavior change to any live accessor

acceptance: `builtin_kind::qualified` is gone from src/builtin_kind.rs, along with its now-orphaned inline test. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green (no unused-code warning survives).
notes: Found sweeping the `provider` subsystem (posture rotation: judges done, provider this tick). Disjoint from every other currently-open queue entry's files — EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE also edits builtin_kind.rs (a doc-comment requalify at `features`, no line overlap) but sits blockedBy a chain, never open alongside this one. scoped at 06fcdb2.

summary: src/drift.rs walks the committed lock's declaration rows three separate times (read_prior_provenance, config_stale, emit_owned_targets) — consolidate to one shared raw-row walk

edit.description: Three fns duplicate the same 'walk the committed lock's declaration rows' job, each independently opening `lock.toml`, tolerant-parsing it as `DocumentMut`, ranging over every top-level `(kind, item)` pair, filtering to `item.as_array_of_tables()`, and pulling columns via `row.get(..).and_then(Item::as_str)`: `read_prior_provenance` (fn 1918, loop 1928-1946, requires name+source_path+emit_hash, returns `Vec<ProvenanceRow>`), `config_stale` (fn 2342, loop 2355-2384, same three columns, inlines the staleness diagnostic instead of returning rows), `emit_owned_targets` (fn 2614, loop 2624-2641, only name+source_path, returns `Vec<EmitOwnedEntry>`). Their doc comments already cross-reference each other's tolerant-absence behavior (1916-1917, 2611-2612) — the duplication is self-aware in the code but never consolidated. Add a private `fn walk_lock_rows(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Vec<RawLockRow>` (raw, all-`Option` columns: kind, name, source_path, emit_hash) that opens and parses the lock once and does the `(kind, item)`/`as_array_of_tables` walk once; have each of the three public fns `filter_map` over its rows instead of re-deriving the walk — `config_stale`'s and `emit_owned_targets`' looser column requirements fall out of a narrower filter each. Same-file fn-level unification — the home stays `drift.rs`, this is not a new module. Consumers (`src/install.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `tests/install.rs`) call only the three public fns — no signature change, no ripple. Re-verified at HEAD 7132213 (unmoved since b8fc7ca, when observed).

tests.asserts: the config_stale(...).is_empty() assertions (536, 607) stay green — the shared walk's config_stale filter/map preserves the same tolerant-absence behavior

re_emitting_after_a_member_is_removed_leaves_a_hand_edited_projection_and_reports_drift and the_emit_report_distinguishes_reaped_from_drifted_orphan stay green — these exercise read_prior_provenance/emit_owned_targets through the reap path with byte-identical output

acceptance: read_prior_provenance, config_stale, and emit_owned_targets all filter/map over one shared walk_lock_rows helper; no behavior change (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': a second implementation of one job is residue, subtracted rather than carried). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Drained from .flume/refactor/plan-drift-lock-row-walk-triplication.md (observed b8fc7ca). Gate changed open -> blockedBy DISCOVERY-INFALLIBLE-RESULT-COLLAPSE: no direct file overlap, but PLACEMENT-MODULE-EXTRACTION/EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE (0040) touch drift.rs downstream and share files with DISCOVERY too — one chain keeps every same-file pair from racing open (pending-entry.md). Re-verified unmoved at f404e48. scoped at f404e48.

summary: emit() parses lock.toml twice per run — read_prior_provenance (reap-diff) and read_declarations (layer-drop check) each independently open+parse it; hoist to one shared parse

per.section: Cost scale is hoisted, and pinned by count

edit.description: `emit` (fn 901) reads and parses the workspace's `lock.toml` twice in one invocation: the reap-diff loop (1197) calls `read_prior_provenance` (fn 1918, opens+parses the lock at 1919-1925 fresh) and the layer-drop check (1244-1247) calls `read_declarations` (fn 3398, opens+parses the lock again at 3399-3407 via `parse_declarations` -> the private `declarations_from_doc` at 3439, which already takes a parsed `&DocumentMut`). Two independent reads+parses of the same file in one `emit()` run — the whole-input work the cost doctrine hoists once per run and shares, never recomputed per call site. Land after DRIFT-LOCK-ROW-WALK-CONSOLIDATION: that entry's `walk_lock_rows` establishes the doc-based-extractor shape this entry's provenance-row side should follow rather than re-deriving. Fix: parse the lock document once inside `emit()`, then derive the reap-diff provenance rows and the layer-drop nested-member counts from that one parsed `DocumentMut` — `declarations_from_doc` already accepts one; give the provenance side an equivalent doc-scoped extractor. Re-verified at HEAD c2eca2d (drift.rs unchanged since b8fc7ca).

tests.asserts: an emit run over a fixture whose lock.toml exercises both the reap-diff and layer-drop-check paths reads/parses lock.toml exactly once — a count pin, the same doctrine this file's discovery-walk/glob-compile pins already established, never a wall-clock threshold

acceptance: emit() reads and parses the workspace's lock.toml exactly once per invocation; the reap-diff provenance rows and the layer-drop nested-member count both derive from that one parse. No behavior change (engineering.md, 'Cost scale is hoisted': whole-input work computes once per run and is shared, never recomputed per call site). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping `drift` (rotation's first subsystem). Distinct from DRIFT-LOCK-ROW-WALK-CONSOLIDATION: that entry unifies three check/install-time readers of provenance columns; this is emit()'s own double-parse of the whole lock document (provenance rows vs the `[declaration]` sub-table) — different row families, same file, same run. Serialized after it so this entry's doc-based provenance extractor sits beside `walk_lock_rows`, not duplicating its shape. scoped at c2eca2d.

summary: 0040's drift→install edge dissolves — the managed-metadata marker vocabulary moves from install.rs to a new flat `placement` pipeline module; drift and install both import it downward

new.description: New flat pipeline-subsystem module (0040, decision point 1; architecture.md's pipeline codemap gains `placement`). Carries the recognizer vocabulary presently in src/install.rs: the three marker constants `MODELINE_MARKER` (156), `NOTE_MARKER` (145), `BANNER_MARKER` (151), plus `is_placement_comment` (1687-1690) and the pub(crate) `placement_lines` (1664-1682) — moved verbatim, `pub(crate)` preserved on `placement_lines` since `drift::emit_one` calls it across the module boundary. `GUARD_MARKER`/`GUARD_MATCHER`/`GUARD_COMMAND`/`GUARD_PATH_MATCH`/`NOTE_COMMENT`/`NOTE_BANNER` stay in install.rs — they are the guard's own detection constants and the placing wording, not the managed-metadata recognizer vocabulary this module owns. The inline test `placement_lines_round_trips_the_body_banner_of_a_frontmatterless_source` (install.rs:1892-1899) moves here with it.

edit.description: Remove the three moved constants (145,151,156), `placement_lines` (1664-1682), and `is_placement_comment` (1687-1690) plus their moved test — replace with `use crate::placement::{MODELINE_MARKER, NOTE_MARKER, BANNER_MARKER};` (or `crate::placement::`-qualified calls). `project_modeline` (1574), `project_note` (1604), and `project_banner` (1632) keep their placing logic and wording (`NOTE_COMMENT` 161, `NOTE_BANNER` 169) but read the three markers off the new module — install imports the vocabulary downward, exactly as drift's emit does (0040).

`emit_one`'s placement read (2034, `install::placement_lines`) becomes `crate::placement::placement_lines`; the `use crate::install;` import (30) — drift.rs's only other reference to `install` is the prose-only doc pointer at 2603 (`install::matches_projection`, unrelated to the moved vocabulary; `matches_projection` stays in install, used by the guard) — drops once the real call moves, dissolving the `drift → install` edge architecture.md declares (0040). Leave the 2603 doc pointer as prose (no code dependency), rewording only if it reads stale once the import is gone; say which in the commit body.

tests.asserts: the moved placement_lines/is_placement_comment tests (round-trips a frontmatterless banner, empty on an unmarked body) pass unchanged from their new home
existing modeline/note/banner placement + config_stale/gate_installed coverage stays green — the move is location-only, no behavior change

acceptance: `src/drift.rs` carries no `crate::install` import; the managed-metadata marker vocabulary lives in a new flat `src/placement.rs` (pipeline subsystem, architecture.md's codemap gains it); install imports the vocabulary downward for its own placing functions. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Decision 0040, point 1. Serialized behind DRIFT-EMIT-LOCK-PARSE-HOIST (behind DRIFT-LOCK-ROW-WALK-CONSOLIDATION, behind DISCOVERY-INFALLIBLE-RESULT-COLLAPSE) for shared-file safety (pending-entry.md, 'Disjoint, or serialized'): this and EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE both touch drift.rs, and DISCOVERY touches install.rs (here) and json_manifest.rs/main.rs (next) — one chain avoids two open entries racing a file. No functional dependency among the four. scoped at f404e48.

summary: extract.rs's upward imports into drift/kind dissolve — lock-row lifters move to drift.rs, the manifest-collection grammar moves to json_manifest.rs; extract returns foundation-clean (0040)

edit.description: Move `nested_members_from_rows` (764-772) and `embedded_member_from_row` (778-798, takes `crate::drift::NestedMemberRow`) down to `src/drift.rs`, which defines the row type. Move the manifest-collection grammar's both faces — `manifest_members` (1015-1040, branches on `crate::kind::CollectionKeyPath` at 1022/1032), `entry_fields` (1048-1056), `enablement_member_fields` (1075-1080, reads `crate::kind::ENABLEMENT_FIELD`), `enablement_entry_value` (1092-1098), `hook_member_fields` (1107-1135), `hook_matcher_group` (1144-1163) — down to `src/json_manifest.rs`, which already imports `crate::extract` for this exact grammar (json_manifest.rs:33,386,398) and owns the format's read+write faces the same way `frontmatter.rs` owns its own (0040, decision point 3, 'a format owns its grammar's read and write faces'). Their inline tests (`nested_members_from_rows_matches_only_the_named_host_address`/`_lifts_leaves_and_one_layer_of_collections` ~1525-1541; `manifest_members_walk_each_entry_raw_and_skip_a_non_object`, `enablement_members_carry_the_scalar_value_and_re_render_identically`, `an_enablement_member_with_no_enabled_field_renders_the_documented_true`, `hook_members_decompose_the_matcher_group_array_and_re_nest_identically` ~1362-1450) move with their functions. After both moves extract.rs imports no sibling module — `use std::...`/`serde_json` only — restoring the module header's own longstanding, narrower claim (22-23, 'no dependency on crate::contract') as the full foundation invariant architecture.md states.

Gains `nested_members_from_rows`/`embedded_member_from_row` from extract.rs, `pub(crate)` (matches today's visibility — `builtin_kind::features` at builtin_kind.rs:578 calls it across the module boundary as `crate::drift::nested_members_from_rows` once moved).

Gains `manifest_members`/`entry_fields`/`enablement_member_fields`/`enablement_entry_value`/`hook_member_fields`/`hook_matcher_group` from extract.rs. Per the reconciliation decision 0040 names for the (now-superseded) EXTRACT-PRIVATE-COLLECTION-DECODERS entry: `manifest_members`, `entry_fields`, `hook_member_fields`, `enablement_member_fields` go fully private `fn` (called only in-module, by `manifest_members` at its own call site 386 and by each other) — none has a caller outside this file once the move lands; `enablement_entry_value`/`hook_matcher_group` stay `pub(crate)` since `crate::drift` calls them across the module boundary (drift.rs:1000,1014 — update the qualification from `crate::extract::` to `crate::json_manifest::`). Drop the now-local `extract::` qualification at the call site (386); the `crate::extract::{self, FeatureValue}` import (33) still needs `extract::json_to_feature` (398), so it narrows to `FeatureValue` plus a bare `extract` import for that one call — verify before narrowing.

`features` (578, doc comment 561) calls `extract::nested_members_from_rows` — requalify to `crate::drift::nested_members_from_rows` (or `drift::` if already imported) once the fn moves; reword the doc pointer at 561 to match.
`ENABLEMENT_FIELD`'s doc comment (214, '`crate::extract::manifest_members`') points at the pre-move location — reword to `crate::json_manifest::manifest_members`.
`nested_member_admissibility`'s doc comment (2068, '`extract::nested_members_from_rows`') points at the pre-move location — reword to name `drift::nested_members_from_rows`.

tests.asserts: the moved nested_members_from_rows tests (host-address matching, one-layer collection lift) pass unchanged from their new home

the moved manifest-grammar tests (raw entry walk skipping non-objects, enablement scalar round-trip, no-enabled-field defaulting true, hook matcher-group decompose/re-nest) pass unchanged from their new home
the composed-corpus check/emit snapshot stays byte-identical — the moves are signature/location-only, no behavior change to any manifest or nested-member read

acceptance: extract.rs imports no crate-internal sibling (foundation-clean); the lock-row lifters live in drift.rs and the manifest-collection grammar (both read and write faces) lives in json_manifest.rs, with manifest_members/entry_fields/hook_member_fields/enablement_member_fields private and enablement_entry_value/hook_matcher_group pub(crate) for drift — reconciling the narrower EXTRACT-PRIVATE-COLLECTION-DECODERS ask at the functions' new homes rather than at extract (0040, Consequences). architecture.md's foundation invariant holds without qualification. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Decision 0040, point 3 — supersedes/retires EXTRACT-PRIVATE-COLLECTION-DECODERS: its enablement_member_fields/hook_member_fields private-fn ask folds into this entry's acceptance at the functions' new home, json_manifest.rs, per 0040's Consequences naming that reconciliation. Serialized behind PLACEMENT-MODULE-EXTRACTION for shared drift.rs edits (pending-entry.md) — also shares json_manifest.rs/main.rs with DISCOVERY-INFALLIBLE-RESULT-COLLAPSE, upstream in the chain. scoped at f404e48.

summary: gate() and explain() each re-read-and-reparse lock.toml several times per invocation via source_deps' own independent parse — hoist to the already-parsed declarations document

per.section: Cost scale is hoisted, and pinned by count

edit.description: `source_deps` (2472) does its own `fs::read_to_string` + `.parse::<DocumentMut>()` of `lock.toml`, independent of `read_declarations`'s (3398) own full parse via `declarations_from_doc` (3439, which already takes a parsed `&DocumentMut` — the doc-scoped-extractor shape DRIFT-EMIT-LOCK-PARSE-HOIST establishes for the provenance side, upstream in this chain). `source_deps` is called by `layout_imports` (2497), `includes` (2508), and — via `source_dep_stale` (2522) — by `layout_import_stale` (2563) and `include_stale` (2580): four independent re-reads/re-parses on top of `read_declarations`'s own. Within one `gate()` invocation (src/main.rs) this lands as 5 full parses of the same file in one run: `read_declarations` (main.rs:858), `layout_imports`+`includes` via `import_edges_from_lock` (main.rs:891), then `layout_import_stale`+`include_stale` (main.rs:1208-1209) — each a fresh read+parse; `explain()` (main.rs:513, 525, 587) repeats the same pattern minus the two staleness calls. `Declarations` (struct 2668) does not currently carry the `layout_import`/`include` lock families read back from disk — `includes: Vec<IncludeRow>` (2691) is documented seam-inbound-only, 'never written back into this declaration table, so a lock round-trip reads it empty' — which is why `source_deps` re-parses independently rather than filtering an already-parsed set. Fix: extend `declarations_from_doc` (or a parallel doc-scoped extractor, the same pattern DRIFT-EMIT-LOCK-PARSE-HOIST establishes) to lift the `[declaration.layout_import]`/`[declaration.include]` families from the one already-parsed document, and have `layout_imports`/`includes`/`source_dep_stale` filter that in-memory set instead of each independently opening and parsing the file. Land after EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE, the last entry in the existing chain to touch this file, so this entry's extractor sits beside the settled post-move shape rather than racing it.

`gate()` (847) and `explain()` (513) each call `drift::read_declarations` once and then separately call `import_edges_from_lock` (2563, calling `drift::layout_imports` then `drift::includes`) — two more independent parses on top of the first — and `gate()` additionally calls `drift::layout_import_stale`/`drift::include_stale` (1208-1209), two more still. Once `source_deps`'s callees read off the shared parsed document instead, thread the already-parsed document/`Declarations` value through these call sites rather than re-deriving it — the same one-parse-per-run shape `read_declarations`'s own call already establishes for the rest of the declaration family.

tests.asserts: a check run over a fixture whose lock.toml carries layout-import and include source-dependency rows reads/parses lock.toml exactly once per gate() invocation — a count pin, the same doctrine this file's discovery-walk/glob-compile/lock-row-walk pins already establish
existing layout-import-stale / include-stale drift findings stay byte-identical — the hoist is signature-only, no behavior change to the staleness comparison

acceptance: gate() and explain() each parse the workspace's lock.toml exactly once per invocation; layout_imports/includes/layout_import_stale/include_stale all derive from that one parse rather than each independently re-reading the file. No behavior change (engineering.md, 'Cost scale is hoisted'). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping `pipeline` (posture rotation, after formats). Distinct from DRIFT-LOCK-ROW-WALK-CONSOLIDATION (top-level [[<kind>]] provenance walk) and DRIFT-EMIT-LOCK-PARSE-HOIST (emit()'s own double-parse) — this is the source_deps family (layout_import/include) on the gate()/explain() read path, unnamed by either. Serialized behind EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE, last in-chain entry touching both drift.rs and main.rs. scoped at 20b7b21.

summary: gate() recomputes the edge-resolution walk 2-4x per invocation — check's own copy plus resolved_edges/resolved_arcs re-derived by acyclic/degree/mention_reachable — consolidate to one shared walk

per.section: Cost scale is hoisted, and pinned by count

edit.description: `check` (pub fn, 111-129) is a from-scratch copy of the admissible-edge x source x target walk, producing dangling-route `Diagnostic`s. `resolved_edges` (pub(crate), 967-990) is the identical iteration shape producing `Vec<ResolvedEdge>`; `resolved_arcs` (private, 1001-1010) folds a fresh `resolved_edges` call (1006) into adjacency. `acyclic` (195-211) calls `resolved_arcs` at 196 (always-on); `degree` (236-...) calls it again at 252 (opt-in); `mention_reachable` (355-...) calls `resolved_edges` directly at 371 (opt-in) — so one `gate()` invocation recomputes the same walk 2 (check + acyclic, always-on) to 4 times (adding degree/mention_reachable when their selections opt in). Change `resolved_arcs` to fold an already-computed `&[ResolvedEdge]` slice into adjacency instead of calling `resolved_edges` itself (drop the call at 1006); change `acyclic`, `degree`, and `mention_reachable`'s signatures to accept the shared `&[ResolvedEdge]` (computed once by `gate()`) rather than `edges`/`by_kind`, folding/filtering over it. `check` needs the *unresolved* half `resolved_edges` discards (a dangling route is exactly what it skips) — the real design decision, left as a judgment call for build (name the choice in the commit body): either (a) widen `resolved_edges` to return both the resolved set and the dangling occurrences in one pass (a `(Vec<ResolvedEdge>, Vec<Diagnostic>)` or small struct), retiring `check`'s separate loop in favor of reading its half off the shared pass, or (b) give `check` its own filter over a richer per-target enumeration `resolved_edges` derives from, keeping it a thin projection rather than a parallel walk. Whichever shape lands, reword `resolved_edges`'s doc comment (964-966) and the module header's READ-EDGE-UNIFY claim (11-13, 'one resolved-edge enumeration... shared') to state the runtime-sharing this consolidation establishes, not just the type-sharing it already had.

`gate()` (847) calls `graph::check` (1131, always-on), `graph::acyclic` (1147, always-on), `graph::degree` (1154, opt-in), and `graph::mention_reachable` (1162, opt-in) — four independent triggers of graph.rs's walk today. Once graph.rs's functions take the shared `&[ResolvedEdge]` slice (or the widened resolved+dangling shape), compute it once here, after `graph::admissibility` (1130) and before the four consumers, and thread it into each call, replacing the `edges`/`by_kind` pair those calls pass today wherever the walk itself moved to the caller.

`why`'s narration (`resolved` at 335) calls `graph::resolved_edges(edges, by_kind)` directly — a separate verb invocation from `gate()`'s own run, so it is not part of the within-gate duplication this entry fixes and needs no consolidation of its own. It is `resolved_edges`'s only consumer outside graph.rs (doc-comment cites at 21, 62, 309, 470) — if the design decision above widens `resolved_edges`'s return shape, this call site's usage narrows to the resolved half; verify at implementation time whether the signature change ripples here, and update the call plus the four doc-comment cites to match whatever shape lands.

tests.asserts: a fixture whose selections carry both a degree and a mention-reachable clause (exercising all four graph.rs call sites) asserts the resolved-edge walk runs exactly once per gate() invocation — a new count-pin instrumented the way import::walk_count()/kind::glob_compile_count() already are in this file
existing acyclic/degree/mention_reachable/check inline tests pass calling the consolidated functions over the shared slice — signature-only change, no diagnostic output changes

acceptance: gate() computes the admissible-edge x source x target walk exactly once per invocation; acyclic, degree, and mention_reachable derive their adjacency/edge set from that one shared computation instead of each independently recomputing via resolved_edges/resolved_arcs, and check's dangling-route diagnostics derive from the same shared pass. A count-pin test proves it. No behavior change (engineering.md, 'Cost scale is hoisted and pinned by count'). `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Drained from .flume/refactor/plan-graph-resolved-edge-walk-duplication.md (observed e2325df, filed sweeping judges' graph.rs). Re-verified unmoved at fa9c87f (git log e2325df..HEAD -- src/ empty). Output shape is a judgment call for build, named in the commit body. Serialized behind DRIFT-SOURCE-DEP-PARSE-HOIST, the last main.rs-touching link in the existing DISCOVERY chain, for shared-file safety (pending-entry.md) — no functional dependency. scoped at fa9c87f.

summary: kind.rs ships three pub fns with zero callers anywhere outside their own file — Commitment::label, Content::label, CustomKind::qualified_name — prune per the export-earns-its-consumer bar

edit.description: Three `pub` items grep-verified zero-consumer (no `.label()`/`.qualified_name()` call anywhere in src/, tests/, or sdk/ outside kind.rs's own inline tests): `impl Commitment { pub fn label(self) -> &'static str { .. } }` (58-68) — the whole `impl Commitment` block, since `label` is its only method; `impl Content { pub fn label(&self) -> &'static str { .. } }` (165-176) — likewise the whole block, `label` is its only method; `CustomKind::qualified_name` (934-939, the doc comment plus `pub fn qualified_name(&self) -> String { self.name.clone() } `) inside the larger `impl CustomKind` block — remove only this method, the surrounding impl block has other live methods. Each type (`Commitment`, `Content`, `CustomKind`) stays; only the dead method/impl goes.

tests.asserts: the now-orphaned inline test `qualified_name_is_the_bare_name` (1826-1830) is removed alongside the fn it exercises; every other kind.rs test (Commitment/Content coverage via their variants, not their removed label methods) stays green

acceptance: `Commitment::label`, `Content::label`, and `CustomKind::qualified_name` are gone from src/kind.rs, along with their now-vacuous inline test. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green (no unused-code warning survives).
notes: Found sweeping 'model' subsystem (posture rotation). Serialized behind EXTRACT-FOUNDATION-BOUNDARY-RESTORE for shared-file safety (pending-entry.md, 'Disjoint, or serialized') — that entry also edits kind.rs (a doc-comment reword at ENABLEMENT_FIELD, no line overlap with this entry's targets, but the file-level rule binds regardless of line overlap). scoped at 4adb1fb.

summary: declared_keys falls through `_ => None` instead of naming every Predicate variant like neighbors target/documented_field do — a new key-declaring variant would silently vanish from the allow-list

per.section: A shared concept is one type

edit.description: `declared_keys` (736-746) matches `Predicate::Required { field } | Predicate::Optional { field } => Some(field)` then falls through `_ => None` for every other variant. Two functions immediately above it over the same enum — `target` (642-673) and `documented_field` (683-720ish) — instead list every non-mapped `Predicate` variant explicitly in the `None` arm's pipe-group (engineering.md's own precedent: 'a `_` arm over a shared concept is a seam defect waiting for its field report'); a future key-declaring variant added to `Predicate` would compile silently against `declared_keys`'s wildcard while `target`/`documented_field` would force a decision. Rewrite `declared_keys`'s match to mirror `target`/`documented_field`'s style: keep the `Required`/`Optional` arm producing `Some(field)`, replace `_ => None` with every remaining variant named in one pipe-grouped arm mapping to `None` (the same variant list `target` already spells, since no other predicate declares a schema key).

tests.asserts: a_member_carrying_only_declared_keys_holds and the file's other closed-keys coverage stay green — the rewrite is exhaustiveness-only, declared_keys returns the identical set for every existing predicate

acceptance: declared_keys's match names every Predicate variant explicitly, no `_` wildcard remains, matching the exhaustive style of target/documented_field two functions above it. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Found sweeping 'model' subsystem (posture rotation). Serialized behind KIND-ZERO-CONSUMER-EXPORTS-PRUNE purely for chain linearity with CONTRACT-REQUIRE-SECTIONS-ROUNDTRIP next (both touch contract.rs, and the schema's single-tag blockedBy can't express 'wait for two independent priors' — same tradeoff DRIFT-LOCK-ROW-WALK-CONSOLIDATION's chain already accepted). No functional dependency on the kind.rs entry. scoped at 4adb1fb.

summary: Predicate::RequireSections can't be authored or decoded — the SDK constructor takes no sections arg, ClauseRow has no column for it, predicate_from_row has no arm — though engine.rs fully judges it

edit.description: `requireSections = (): Predicate => ({ key: "require_sections" })` (168) takes no argument even though its own doc comment reads 'The named headings are present' — unlike `dependencyExists` (whose missing constructor is explicitly documented: 'the engine holds it back absent a decidable reference syntax'), nothing here names this as a deliberate hold. Change the signature to `requireSections = (sections: readonly string[]): Predicate => ({ key: "require_sections", sections })`. Add a `sections?: readonly string[]` field to the `Predicate` interface (76, beside `section`), documented the same way `keys`/`values` are ('`require_sections`'s required heading list').

`clauseRow` (65-115) maps each `Predicate` field to its `ClauseRow` column by explicit `predicate.key === "..."` branches (`keys`, `values`, `section`, etc. — no generic spread). Add: `sections: predicate.key === "require_sections" && predicate.sections ? [...predicate.sections] : undefined,` alongside the existing `keys`/`values`/`section` branches (110-114), copying into a fresh array per the file's own documented reason ('the generated rows carry mutable columns; the predicate's ... is read-only, so copy each into a fresh array').

`ClauseRow` (struct at 2869-2971) carries no column a `require_sections` row could use — `keys`/`values`/`section` are documented as each other predicate's own. Add a new field `pub sections: Option<Vec<String>>,` after `section` (2970), with a doc comment naming it `require_sections`'s required-heading-list column, matching the `#[serde(default)]` pattern every other optional column carries. This regenerates `sdk/src/generated/ClauseRow.ts` via the existing ts-rs export test — do not hand-edit the generated file.

`predicate_from_row` (424-...) has arms for every predicate key except `require_sections` and the constructor-less `dependency-exists` (falls through `_ => return None`). Add, near the `must_define` (458) / `section_contains` (461) arms: `"require_sections" => Predicate::RequireSections { sections: row.sections.clone()? },`.

tests.asserts: a new test asserts requireSections(["Usage", "Decision"]) composes { key: "require_sections", sections: ["Usage", "Decision"] } as an ordinary Predicate value, the pattern the file's other node-set constructor tests (count, unique, membership) already follow

a new (or extended) round-trip test pushes a require_sections ClauseRow with a sections column through emit + read_declarations and asserts predicate_from_row lifts it back to Predicate::RequireSections { sections } with the same list, the same shape a_floor_clause_row_round_trips_its_node_scope_predicate_argument already proves for forbidden_keys/allowed_chars

acceptance: An SDK author can call requireSections([...]) with real heading names, the emitted lock round-trips them through a new sections ClauseRow column, and predicate_from_row decodes a require_sections row back into Predicate::RequireSections { sections } — closing the one predicate in the closed vocabulary whose lock declaration rows (pipeline.md, 'The lock') could not carry its own argument. `cargo test` (Rust) and `pnpm --dir sdk test` (SDK gate) green.
notes: Found sweeping 'model' via predicate_from_row. DependencyExists's parallel missing arm is NOT part of this entry — its SDK constructor is deliberately absent (contract.ts says why), genuinely unreachable, unlike RequireSections's unusable half-built constructor. Serialized behind CONTRACT-DECLARED-KEYS-EXHAUSTIVE-MATCH for shared contract.rs/drift.rs safety. scoped at 4adb1fb.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (630) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (657), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 528 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 626-629 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 652-655 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) shifted every address after ~519 — the const (59) and its doc (55-58) sit above the edit and are unmoved; the later addresses re-read at HEAD, so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1407) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1408-1412) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1410) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) added the mention-reachable fixture and shifted this test down; addresses re-read at HEAD, so they carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at d1af9a5 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; VERIFIER-TYPED's window touched graph.rs but not the const region (still 5 at graph.rs:59, cite still 2026-07-02 at 55-58). summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at d1af9a5, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 68fae5c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
